description: Wire production database drivers, harden DSN construction, create a composition root with lazy initialization, and connect default store factories to all cloud and auth commands so they work against real Turso/Postgres backends.

userStories.title: Register production database drivers via blank imports
userStories.description: As a deployer, I want libsql and pgx drivers registered at init time so that sql.Open calls resolve without runtime errors.
acceptanceCriteria: A new file internal/cloud/deploy/drivers.go contains blank imports for github.com/tursodatabase/libsql-client-go/libsql and github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/stdlib
go.mod includes the libsql-client-go dependency after running go mod tidy
sql.Drivers() includes both 'libsql' and 'pgx' when drivers.go is imported
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add ValidateStore method to Config
userStories.description: As a command author, I want a ValidateStore method that checks only database-related env vars so that commands not needing runner credentials can validate the store config independently.
acceptanceCriteria: Config has a new ValidateStore() error method in internal/cloud/deploy/config.go
ValidateStore checks TursoURL and TursoAuthToken when adapter is turso
ValidateStore checks PostgresDSN when adapter is postgres
ValidateStore does NOT check RunnerURL or RunnerServiceToken
Existing Validate() method remains unchanged
Unit test covers turso-missing-url, turso-missing-token, postgres-missing-dsn, and valid cases
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Harden Turso DSN construction with net/url
userStories.description: As a deployer, I want the Turso DSN to be constructed using net/url so that special characters in the auth token are properly escaped and existing query parameters in the URL are preserved.
acceptanceCriteria: openTurso in internal/cloud/deploy/store_factory.go uses net/url.Parse to build the DSN
Auth token is set via url.Values rather than string concatenation
If TursoURL already contains query parameters, authToken is appended without clobbering them
Unit test verifies DSN output for a URL with no query params
Unit test verifies DSN output for a URL that already has query params
Unit test verifies special characters in auth token are escaped
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create cloud runtime composition root with lazy init
userStories.description: As a command author, I want a DefaultStoreFactory function that loads config from env, validates it, and opens the store once using sync.Once so that multiple commands share a single connection without repeated setup.
acceptanceCriteria: A new file internal/cloud/deploy/runtime.go exports DefaultStoreFactory() (cloud.Store, error)
Internally uses sync.Once to ensure LoadConfigFromEnv, ValidateStore, and OpenStore run exactly once
Returns the cached store on subsequent calls
On failure, DefaultStoreFactory returns an error that wraps the underlying ValidateStore or OpenStore error (verifiable in tests)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add runtime unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests for the runtime composition root so that lazy initialization and error paths are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file internal/cloud/deploy/runtime_test.go exists
Test verifies DefaultStoreFactory returns same store instance on multiple calls (sync.Once behavior)
Test verifies error is returned when required env vars are missing
Tests use LoadConfig with custom getenv to avoid real env dependency
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire default store factories for cloud commands
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want cloud submit, status, logs, cancel, run, and pull commands to use DefaultStoreFactory when no test override is set so that commands work against real databases without manual wiring.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of the 6 cloud command factory variables (cloudSubmitStoreFactory, cloudStatusStoreFactory, cloudLogsStoreFactory, cloudCancelStoreFactory, cloudRunStoreFactory, cloudPullStoreFactory) is set to deploy.DefaultStoreFactory in an init() block in cmd/cloud.go when it is nil
The if-nil guard pattern preserves test override capability
cloudSubmitConfigFactory and cloudRunConfigFactory remain nil (no default config factory needed)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire default store factories for auth commands
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want cloud auth link, import, status, validate, and revoke commands to use DefaultStoreFactory when no test override is set so that auth commands work against real databases.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of the 5 auth command factory variables (cloudAuthLinkStoreFactory, cloudAuthImportStoreFactory, cloudAuthStatusStoreFactory, cloudAuthValidateStoreFactory, cloudAuthRevokeStoreFactory) is set to deploy.DefaultStoreFactory in an init() block in cmd/cloud_auth.go when it is nil
The if-nil guard pattern preserves test override capability
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Load dotenv in cloud env command
userStories.description: As a deployer, I want the cloud env command to load a .env file from the deploy directory before validating so that I can validate my .env file without exporting variables manually.
acceptanceCriteria: go.mod includes github.com/joho/godotenv dependency
runCloudEnv in cmd/cloud_deploy.go calls godotenv.Load before LoadConfig, ignoring file-not-found errors
When .env file exists, its variables are available via os.Getenv during validation
When .env file does not exist, command still works with existing env vars
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update deploy/.env.example with complete key set
userStories.description: As a deployer, I want the .env.example to include HAL_CLOUD_RUNNER_URL and document all keys so that I have a complete reference when configuring the deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: deploy/.env.example contains HAL_CLOUD_RUNNER_URL with a placeholder value

All 6 env var keys from deploy/config.go constants are present: HAL_CLOUD_DB_ADAPTER, HAL_CLOUD_TURSO_URL, HAL_CLOUD_TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN, HAL_CLOUD_POSTGRES_DSN, HAL_CLOUD_RUNNER_URL, HAL_CLOUD_RUNNER_SERVICE_TOKEN
DAYTONA_API_KEY and DAYTONA_SERVER_URL remain in the file
Each variable has a comment describing its purpose
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Targeted verification for DB runtime wiring
userStories.description: As a developer, I want targeted verification for DB runtime wiring so that config validation, store factory wiring, and cloud command behavior are confirmed without requiring unrelated full-suite work.
acceptanceCriteria: go test ./internal/cloud/deploy/... exits 0 with no failures
go test ./cmd -run 'TestRunCloud|TestRunCloudAuth|TestRunCloudEnv' exits 0
go build ./... exits 0
go vet ./... exits 0
Typecheck passes
